column1: # Principle: `null` and the None bottom type.
# AQL's type lattice has an explicit None type at one of the two top-
# level "bottoms": None (the unit type — exactly one inhabitant) and
# Never (the uninhabited type). The spec tokenizer maps the bare
# token `null` to a None type-literal value, so `null` is the
# user-facing way to spell that single inhabitant.
# Rules being asserted
# 1. The bare token `null` parses to a None-typed Value with Data=nil
#    (a "type literal" — the type *is* the value).
# 2. The renderer prints any None-typed nil-Data value as `null`.
# 3. None matches no concrete-typed sig: feeding `null` to a sig that
#    expects Integer / String / Boolean / List raises signature_error.
# 4. None DOES match a sig declared with Any. The `tagq` test word's
#    [Any] catch-all is the canonical witness; passing `null` lands
#    there and returns "any".
# 5. None survives stack residency unchanged: a `null` left on the
#    stack at end-of-Run renders as `null`.
# 6. `firstq` on an empty list returns `null` — the conventional
#    "no value" sentinel for missing-element queries. (Confirms the
#    handler in spec_test.go::registerSpecWords actually emits a
#    None type literal here.)
# Why this matters
# None is the universal "no result" / "absent" sentinel: it lets
# functions like `first []` return *something* without raising and
# without conflating "empty list returned nothing" with "list has a
# zero element". Sigs that accept None explicitly (or fall back to
# Any) must continue to handle it; sigs that do not must reject it
# cleanly with signature_error rather than panicking on a nil Data
# payload — the panic-prevention rule from CLAUDE.md.
# tokens
null
column3: bare null round-trips as a None type literal

column1: null null
column3: multiple nulls coexist on the stack

column1: 1 null 2
column3: null between integers — none of them interact

column1: tagq null
column3: None-typed value lands at [Any] catch-all overload

column1: addq null 1
column3: addq wants Integer Integer; None fails sig

column1: addq 1 null
column3: null in second forward slot also fails

column1: concatq "a" null
column3: concatq wants String String; None fails

column1: notq null
lengthq null
firstq [ ]
column3: empty list → first returns the None sentinel

column1: lengthq [ null ]
column3: a list containing null is still a 1-element list

column1: firstq [ null 1 ]
column3: first element of a list whose head is null
